title: What’s on deck for markets: Friday, May 1
body: Oops, something went wrong Market Domination host Josh Lipton breaks down what to watch Friday, May 1, including oil giants Exxon XOM ), Chevron ( CVX ), and Colgate-Palmolive ( CL ). Time now for to watch Friday, May 1st. Starting off on the earnings front, some big names in the energy sector, announcing results on Friday including Exon and Chevron, both reporting first quarter results and both facing a similar dynamic. Higher oil prices helping profits, but geopolitical disruption weighing on volumes. For Exon, analyst expecting stronger upstream pricing to add nearly $3 billion to results, but that tailwind being offset by war-related disruptions, maintenance and a fewer operating days. title: Oil climbs, gas hits highest price since 2022
body: Oops, something went wrong Yahoo Finance's Breaking Business News Reporter Jake Conley joins Market Catalyst to discuss the current state of oil ( CL=F ), BZ=F ) as the Iran conflict drags on. Both oil futures are trading over $105 per barrel, as the national average for gas is now $4.30, according to AAA, jumping 30 cents in just one week. As you said, right now, national average, $4.30 a gallon. A week ago, that was just over $4. That is a 30-cent jump in a week, which you do not see in the US gasoline market. You just don't see a jump like that. So, there continues to be this sort of disconnect between that and oil prices where they are and the sort of equity market narrative of like, we're done with the war. Mhm. Right. It's not, you know, War's over. we're closer to the end than the

title: Dow Jones Futures Rise As Apple Climbs On Earnings, Sandisk Skids; Stock Market At High
body: BREAKING: Apple Leads Earnings Movers After S&P 500, Nasdaq Hit Highs Dow Jones futures rose slightly early Friday, along with S&P 500 futures and Nasdaq futures. Apple (AAPL), Sandisk (SNDK), Western Digital (WDC) and Roku (ROKU) all beat earnings views after the close. The stock market rally had a solid session with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq hitting new highs amid massive earnings news and crude oil prices pulling back. title: Indian Oil hikes prices of industrial LPG, jet fuel for foreign airlines
body: Something went wrong May 1 (Reuters) - India's largest state-run refiner Indian Oil Corporation ‌has raised the prices ‌of liquefied petroleum gas used by ​industries and jet fuel for foreign airlines from Friday, the company said in a statement. The ‌price of ⁠a 19-kilogram commercial LPG cylinder for industrial clients ⁠was hiked by 993 rupees, or 47.8%, to 3,071.5 rupees, ​the refiner ​said. Prices of ​household LPG, primarily ‌used as cooking fuel, were left unchanged. Jet fuel prices for domestic airlines were also not revised, the company said. The ‌price hikes come ​amid a sharp ​surge ​in global oil prices, ‌which have climbed above $100 ​a ​barrel following the closure of the Strait of Hormuz amid ​the ‌ongoing Iran war. (Reporting by Kashish ​Tandon in Bengaluru; Editing ​by

title: Trump Says Iran Blockade ‘Incredible’ as Pump Prices Keep Rising
body: Oops, something went wrong (Bloomberg) -- President Donald Trump said he was sticking with a naval blockade of Iranian ports amid concerns the vital Strait of Hormuz would not reopen anytime soon.
